title: [bug] the satellite role heuristic tests a MECHANISM (@dataclass), not the concept — pydantic models score as primary
description:
FIXED + MEASURED 2026-07-19. See CORRECTION at the bottom — the bead's headline claim was itself wrong.

THE FIX: _is_dataclass -> _is_data_container. A data container is identified by being DECLARED, not by
which library declares it: @dataclass (stdlib + pydantic.dataclasses), attrs @define/@frozen/@mutable, and
the BaseModel / NamedTuple / TypedDict bases. The *Cfg naming vocab stays rejected, and there is now a test
pinning that a class merely NAMED Config is still PRIMARY, so the rejection cannot quietly erode.

KNOWN GAP, recorded as a decision rather than an oversight: attrs' legacy @attr.s reduces to the trailing
name `s`, and admitting a bare `s` would match any @x.s -- a worse rule than the gap. Test pins it.

MEASURED, all three consumer repos, their real package lists from .copier-answers.yml:

  repo          classes(old -> new)   demeter   envy(old -> new)   composition
  cardiac-seg      23 -> 16              22        8 -> 1              0
  mindscape        30 -> 13               1       23 -> 0              0
  synthscape       19 -> 11               2        2 -> 2              0

ENVY IS THE REAL WIN and it validates the "one gap, three symptoms" thesis: envy excludes satellites as
targets, so fixing the role fixed envy almost entirely (33 findings -> 3 across all three repos). envy is
ENFORCED, so this is the part that would actually have broken consumers.

GRADUATION VERDICTS (the shape_contracts precedent):
  composition  0/0/0   -> blocks from day one, as planned
  envy         1/0/2   -> 3 findings total; enforceable, they are almost certainly true positives
  demeter      22/1/2  -> separate concern, as this bead always said. cardiac-seg is the outlier and has
                          the per-repo [tool.structure] demeter_max_depth escape. NOT a role problem.
  classes      16/13/11 -> ADVISORY. The survivors are largely GENUINE multi-abstraction files
                          (neuroscan/models/composite.py holds Backbone, Head and Model), i.e. consumer
                          refactoring work, not a scaffold defect.

CORRECTION to this bead's own premise: it projected "class-roles 23/30/14 would fail all three repos", but
`devtools.classes` is wired into ZERO runners -- noxfile, ci.yml and pre-commit all have zero references.
That gate cannot fire, so it could never have failed anyone. The column was measuring an engine no runner
invokes (the same absent-gate shape as iv5). The fix still matters, for a reason the bead did not give:
envy and archmap CONSUME the role classifier, and envy is enforced.

Wiring `classes` (advisory) belongs with iv5, which owns gate-set completeness.
Follow-up filed as dun.1: structural Protocol conformance, measured at ~8 of the 40 survivors.

title: devtools: domain-folder the gates (graph/coupling/cohesion/hygiene/tools) — owner overrides the no-kind-bucket rule
notes: OWNER DECISION (2026-07-22), explicitly overriding yfv/CLAUDE.md's 'no grouping by KIND' for the GATES. The import graph shows the analyzers do not co-fire (they wire to plumbing), so this grouping is by analytical DOMAIN, not co-use — the exact move the yfv bead forbade. The owner chose it anyway as 'obvious to a maintainer' and to empty the root. Recorded so future work does NOT revert it as a principle violation: for GATES, domain grouping is now sanctioned. plumbing/ + the graph subsystem remain genuine co-use. Target: graph/{classes,calls,arrows,fitness(was graph.py),archmap} · coupling/{demeter,envy,composition,contracts,purity} · cohesion/{lcom,data_clumps,state_candidates,complexity} · hygiene/{magic_literals,small,mirror} · tools/{config,analytics} · root{run,__init__,astgrep,shape_contracts} · plumbing/. Every gate path renames (~25 copier re-renders). Ships in PR #31, v1.25.0.
close_reason: Done. Domain-folder reorg shipped: graph/(model+fitness+archmap) coupling/ cohesion/ hygiene/ tools/, root=run+astgrep+shape_contracts, plumbing/ unchanged. graph.py->graph/fitness.py. Every gate path renamed + copier renders updated (noxfile/CI/pre-commit). Fixed 2 __file__-relative resource-locator bugs (config, archmap) by resolving against package root. Widened the gate-wiring consistency test regex for dotted paths. Mirror gate accepted the tree with zero config change. 567 devtools + 8 scaffold pass, nox lint green. Recorded the by-domain override in each folder's __init__ so it is not reverted as a principle violation. e2e running. Ships in PR #31, v1.25.0.

title: roll v1.20.0 into the four consumers and triage what it finds
description:
`copier update` in cardiac-seg, mindscape, synthscape, mirage, then run each repo's gate set and record
what changed. checkpoint, not solo: the update itself is mechanical, but every finding is a judgment —
fix it, accept it, or decide the RULE is wrong. That last one is real and has happened (the satellite
heuristic was wrong, not the repos).

First thing to confirm, because it was broken until today: the pin actually resolves. `uv sync` fetching
`sdlc-devtools @ git+...@v1.20.0` is the end-to-end proof no test in the suite can give — the e2e
deliberately overrides it with a local source.

Expect, roughly: envy findings DROP (satellite fix), `typing.Any` appears advisory, pyrefly now aimed at
the floor each repo declares, and archmap graph.json regenerating with method-level call edges (a large
but mechanical diff — do not read it as drift).

Record per repo: what fired, what was fixed, what was accepted and why.
close_reason:
WRONG FRAMING, withdrawn. This planned to run `copier update` across the consumer repos and triage what
fired there — which is not the scaffold's job.

Each consumer repo has its own agent, and they are the experts on that codebase's integration. They decide
what does not fit and send a ticket BACK here to be considered as a general architecture question. Planning
to roll out and triage inverts that: it treats downstream as a surface to enforce against rather than as
peers reporting upward.

What IS the scaffold's job, and is already done: ship a release that can actually be consumed. The pin
resolves (bd 540), and the template only calls engines the pinned release ships (added with v1.21.0) —
both of which were genuinely broken and are now gated.

Calibrating a NEW analyzer against consumer source trees read-only stays legitimate — that is measurement,
not enforcement, and v3c.1 needs it.

title: epic adoption: prove v1.20.0 in the consumer repos
description:
v1.20.0 is tagged and NOTHING has consumed it. Everything built since v1.19 — the satellite role fix, the
Any ban, pyrefly on the floor it promises, method-level call resolution, the 22-command gate set — has only
ever run against sdlc-devtools itself. The scaffold's whole thesis is that these gates hold on real repos;
until they run on real repos that is an untested claim.

Four consumers, not three: cardiac-seg, mindscape, synthscape, and mirage (mirage adopted v1.15.0 and
produced the acq audit, which is the best evidence so far that outside adoption finds what dogfooding
cannot).

Low sync cost is the premise, and it should be VERIFIED rather than assumed: nothing since v1.19.1 changes
a blocking gate's verdict except the satellite fix, which LOOSENS envy (33 -> 3 findings here). The Any ban
ships advisory. So a `copier update` should be quiet. If it is not, that is the finding.

This epic ends with: four repos on v1.20.0, their findings triaged, and a decision recorded on which
advisories graduate. It also validates the release path end to end — the pin was quietly broken all
session and only a real `copier update` proves it resolves.
close_reason:
Dissolved along with its main child: adoption is not the scaffold's epic to run. See a27.1 — downstream
agents own integration and file tickets upward; the scaffold's remit ends at shipping something coherent
and consumable.

Also corrected on the way out: there are THREE consumers, not four. `mirage` is the local name for what is
`synthscape` on GitHub, and this epic counted it twice.

1a8 (drop the test_ prefix) is unparented rather than closed — it is a real piece of scaffold work. Its
consumer-coordination note stands, but reframed: the scaffold's part is shipping a migration path that
accepts BOTH layouts for a version, so downstream can adopt on their own schedule.

title: epic GOAL: v1.20.0 release-ready — the scaffold passes its own bar
description:
GOAL. Everything the scaffold has built since v1.19.1 sits unreleased on dev: classes, composition,
contracts, envy. None of it reaches a consumer until v1.20.0 is TAGGED, and it must not be tagged while a
known-wrong heuristic would fail all three consumer repos. This goal ends with that tag.

The theme is DOGFOODING: the scaffold does not currently run on itself the gate set it sells. Every defect
found today came from the same shape -- a gate that does not run, a guard that probes something other than
what it guards, a claim asserted from memory instead of checked. So the goal is sequenced to make the tools
trustworthy FIRST, then to point them at ourselves, and only then to ship.

EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE (next PR): the method tier and graph upgrades -- f1u (method-level call resolution
+ public/all depth split), 5cg (shared Resolver), a0a (self-edges). Also 1a8 (test_ prefix migration, needs
its own release for consumer coordination) and f9y (performance, measure-first).

PHASE 1 -- TRUST THE TOOLS (96j, y52)
Cheap, and everything after it depends on the result being believable.
  96j  has_node() probes shutil.which("npx") while subprocess needs npx.cmd, so 3 e2e tests are
       permanently red on Windows. Three tests you are trained to ignore is how a real jscpd regression
       walks in.
  y52  the same class of template/ruff-format bug shipped THREE times because the fast gate only catches
       lines that are too LONG, never lines ruff would JOIN. Render the templates and run ruff format
       --check in the unit suite: milliseconds instead of a 90s e2e round-trip, exact instead of heuristic.

PHASE 2 -- FIX WHAT BLOCKS THE TAG (az9, 166)
  az9  P0. The satellite heuristic tests a MECHANISM (@dataclass) rather than the concept (a declarative
       data container), so pydantic models score as primary -- and the template ships pydantic. Widen to
       BaseModel/attrs/NamedTuple/TypedDict, then RE-MEASURE all three repos. The *Cfg naming vocab is
       rejected; do not re-propose it.
  166  pyrefly missing-override-decorator vs requires-python >=3.11 (typing.override is 3.12+). A shipped
       gate that cannot be satisfied on our own stated floor.

PHASE 3 -- DOGFOOD (iv5, then 0y9)
  iv5  devtools runs a hand-rolled SUBSET: no pyrefly, vulture, jscpd, coverage, and no advisory explorers.
       Run the same gate set we ship. Expect real findings -- pyrefly strict over 17 engines is the big one.
  0y9  one of those findings, and the reason it went unnoticed: jscpd never ran here. 17x duplicated
       argparse plumbing over a half-formed interface (report() on 11, run_assert() on 6, --assert on 7).
       Define the interface FIRST, then the Cli is trivial and if-free. Ordered after iv5 so the gate finds
       it rather than us asserting it.

PHASE 4 -- THE NEW GATE (lph)
  lph  forbid Any: it opts out of the type system we made blocking. TID251 on the import is a complete ban
       (nested `dict[str, Any]` escapes every use-site rule but dies with the import). Two open questions
       to settle BEFORE it ships enforced: does pyrefly strict already reject a bare `dict` return (else
       this is half a rule and the dynamism just relocates), and what do the three consumer repos measure?

PHASE 5 -- RELEASE (ztw, then tag)
  ztw  the README front page still claims v1.2 / feature-complete.
  Then tag v1.20.0, and only then do consumers see any of it.

ACCEPTANCE
  - devtools runs the same gate set it ships, not a subset, with no self-exemption
  - every ENFORCED gate measured against cardiac-seg / mindscape / synthscape BEFORE the tag; anything
    still noisy ships advisory with a written graduation path (the shape_contracts precedent)
  - zero permanently-red tests in a local e2e run
  - v1.20.0 tagged, and the three consumers can copier update onto it without a wall of findings

PROCESS (standing, from this session): dev and main only -- no feature branches. No self-merging; the PR is
the gate. Verify claims by RUNNING them before writing them into a bead.
close_reason: v1.20.0 tagged at 2e71e6f — the acceptance condition. Released by release.yml on merge, not by hand, which also closes the process hole the goal kept tripping over.

title: calls terminate on the method, not the owning class (incl. intra-class)
description:
CallArrows currently aggregates per class, emitting (src_class, dst_class, kind, via). Both endpoints are
already derivable at walk time and are being thrown away:

- TARGET method — the attribute name in `self.repo.save()` IS `save`, in hand when the receiver resolves
- SOURCE method — the enclosing FunctionDef, currently collapsed by the per-class aggregation

RESOLUTION RULE (one rule, every case): the edge points at the class that DEFINES the method, found by
walking the project MRO from the declared receiver type.

- inherited from a project base -> lands on the base's method, where the code actually is
- declared type is a Protocol -> the Protocol defines it, so it lands there. This is not a special case; it
  is the call->interface partition falling out for free
- base is stdlib / third-party (dict.get, BaseModel.model_dump) -> the chain leaves the project, honest
  drop. We do not track outside our own packages, by decision
- dynamic (getattr, kwargs forwarding) -> unresolvable, honest drop (see the getattr gate task)

INTRA-CLASS calls are in scope: receiver is `self`, so the target class is the enclosing class and no
resolution is needed at all. A public method calling its own private helper is real internal structure and
is the reason the `all` depth stop exists. Note these are intra-FILE, so they roll up to a file self-loop
that the import graph legitimately drops — consistent with the roll-up invariant, not a violation of it.

Also sharpen the partition: `calls` -> method, `construct` -> class. Constructing is __init__, i.e. the
class as a whole, so behavioural coupling lands INSIDE the box and wiring lands ON it.

Unblocks the feature-envy method cut, which needs the same per-method resolution.

Removes the claim in archmap.py that method-level edges would be "inventing a precision the graph does not
have" — that comment is now wrong and must go with the change.
close_reason: CallArrows.edges() now returns CallEdge (both method endpoints); Resolver.definer() walks the project MRO to whoever DEFINES the method. construct split out as its own kind (lands on the class — constructing is __init__). class_edges() is the coarse projection, so contracts/graph/composition read what they always did. RESULT on devtools: method tier went 176 nodes / 0 edges -> 213 nodes / 240 method-to-method + 34 construct. Cross-class method edges 2 -> 76 after also resolving two receiver shapes the source states exactly: fluent construction (Trees(pkgs).walk()) and static calls via the class name (Resolver.field_types(...)) — those two were costing most of the connectivity.

title: depth control: split method into public / all stops
description:
DEPTHS becomes ['module', 'class', 'public', 'all'] in devtools/archviz/viewer.js. The scale stays ORDINAL
because public methods are a strict subset of all methods — no new widget, one more stop.

- `public` draws method nodes whose label has no leading underscore (87 of 176 in devtools)
- `all` draws every method node including private and dunder (+71 private, +18 dunder)
- readFilters() maps the stop to the node filter; the method node filter already requires its class present

The emitter needs no change — graph.json already carries every method node. This is viewer-side only.

Update the archmap schema tests: `test_the_method_tier_is_opt_in_in_the_viewer` asserts the DEPTHS literal,
so it must learn the 4-stop scale while still pinning that the view OPENS at `module`.
close_reason: DEPTHS is now ['module','class','public','all'] in viewer.js; readFilters maps the 4th stop to a privates filter and the method node filter drops leading-underscore labels at 'public'. devtools: 213 method nodes, 109 public / 104 non-public. Scale stays ordinal (public is a strict subset of all), so no new widget. test_the_method_tier_is_opt_in_in_the_viewer pins the literal + the module opening.

title: epic method tier: real method-level resolution + public/all depth split
description:
The method tier currently ships NODES ONLY (176 of them in devtools, zero edges among them). That makes it
a wall of labels rather than a graph: opening `method` depth adds detail but no connectivity.

Two objectives, tracked as children:

1. DEPTH SPLIT — `method` becomes two ordinal stops, `public` and `all`. Public methods are a subset of all
   methods, so the containment scale stays ordinal (module > class > public > all) and no new control is
   added. 176 methods split 87 public / 71 private / 18 dunder, so the public stop halves the wall while
   keeping the class's actual SURFACE readable.

2. METHOD-LEVEL BEHAVIOUR ARROWS — `calls` terminates on the method it calls, not on the owning class.
   Includes INTRA-CLASS calls (a public method calling its own private helper), which is where a class's
   internal structure actually lives and which the `all` stop exists to show.

Superseded during design: treating a private method as a method-tier "satellite" (muted, dropped by the
existing hide-satellites toggle). Rejected in favour of the depth split, which keeps the ordinal model and
keeps privates present as edge endpoints.
close_reason: All four children done. The method tier is now a graph rather than a wall of labels: 176 nodes / 0 edges -> 213 nodes / 274 edges, and the depth control gained the public/all split so the tier opens at a class's SURFACE before its internals.

title: GOAL: trustworthy architecture guardrails — typed class-relationship graph + viz
description: LONG GOAL (hand to /goal). Evolve graph.py from an import-only module graph into a typed class-relationship graph over one containment tree (package>file>class>method), arrows at their native level (import@file, inherits/holds@class, calls@method incl via=construct), metrics as edge-subset queries, plus the archmap rendering/diff. WHY: gates that talk about REAL dependencies (imports are noisy) so AI-authored architecture can be trusted because it is machine-enforced. DONE when, on the enriched e2e sample project: (1) containment tree + node roles emitted; (2) all four gated kinds carried as per-edge {kinds,weights}; (3) roll-up property holds (file-level roll-up == grimp import set cross-file; intra-file collapses to dropped self-loops); (4) EVERY new gate both runs CLEAN and is proven to BITE via assert_bites; (5) archmap renders kinds + filters + --diff changelog, graph.json deterministic; (6) every PRE-EXISTING gate stays green on the enriched seed. Spec + e2e acceptance matrix: docs/typed_class_graph_design.md. Children: sdlc-scaffold-4bl (arrows), sdlc-scaffold-433 (viz). Start at sdlc-scaffold-4bl.6 (A0 seed enrichment). NOT in scope: dead-import (ruff F401 + vulture own it).
close_reason: Closed

title: A0 enrich the e2e sample project to exercise every arrow kind + gate
description: PREREQ for the whole epic - the current seed (MathOps+Pipeline, one import edge) has no inheritance/composition/interfaces so it cannot exercise a single new arrow. Grow the e2e-owned seed to: types.py (Store Protocol PRIMARY + StoreConfig dataclass SATELLITE -> roles + intra-file pair), memory_store.py (MemoryStore(Store) -> inherits), repository.py (holds a Store field, calls it -> holds + call-to-INTERFACE), service.py (holds Repository, constructs MemoryStore -> via=construct to CONCRETE), keep math_ops/pipeline. Constraint: the seed must itself pass EVERY existing gate (pyrefly strict typed, in-a-class, test-mirrored, dup-free, vulture-clean, coverage floor) - it doubles as proof the gates coexist. See the e2e acceptance matrix in docs/typed_class_graph_design.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: A2 Batch1: structural arrow decomposition (inherits/holds/constructs/references) one AST pass
description: One AST pass emitting finer arrows at native level: inherits + holds (has-a, field-type annotations) @ class; references @ method (ungated attribute). calls is Batch2. Represent as DiGraph with per-edge {kinds:set,weights} (not MultiDiGraph). constructs folded into calls as via=construct subtag (Batch2). Self-check: cross-file structural arrows roll up to grimp imports; intra-file arrows = file self-loops with no import (expected). Kills import-noise; value without calls.
close_reason: Closed

title: A1 re-key graph nodes module->class + node roles (primary/satellite) + 2-primaries gate
description:
Nodes form a fractal containment tree: package>file>class>method ('everything is a module'). Class is the primary working level; tag roles primary/satellite; two PRIMARY classes in one file = a Structure gate. import graph = the file-level roll-up (self-loops dropped). Data goes to method level; the view folds to file (archmap containment already IS this tree).

ROLE HEURISTIC (constraint found while building the A0 fixture): a satellite is not only a config dataclass/enum/helper - it ALSO includes a same-file SUBCLASS of that file's primary. The A0 seed has errors.py (StoreError + KeyMissingError(StoreError)) and memory_store.py (MemoryStore + CapacityError(StoreError)); an exception hierarchy in one file is idiomatic and must NOT trip the 2-primaries gate. So: classify a class as satellite if it (a) is a dataclass/enum/config-ish, (b) subclasses another class defined in the SAME file, or (c) subclasses a base imported for that purpose (e.g. a module-local error family). Only genuinely independent peers count as primaries.

REUSE: graph.py already has _dotted (base-name resolution), _is_abstract, _module_file, abstractness (ClassDef walk) - the class enumeration builds on those, and lcom.py already collects per-method access sets.
close_reason: Closed

description: LONG GOAL: evolve graph.py from an import-only module graph into a typed class-relationship graph over one containment tree (package>file>class>method), with arrows emitted at their native level (import@file, inherits/holds@class, calls@method) and metrics as edge-subset queries. DONE = on the enriched e2e sample project: containment tree + node roles emitted; all four gated kinds carried as per-edge {kinds,weights}; the roll-up property holds (file-level roll-up == grimp import set cross-file, intra-file collapses to dropped self-loops); EVERY new gate both runs clean AND is proven to BITE via assert_bites; archmap renders kinds/filters/--diff; and every pre-existing gate stays green on the enriched seed. title: self-contained cytoscape HTML viewer (hydrates graph.json)
description: The interactive explorer: vendored cytoscape.js + expand-collapse + fcose (all MIT, inlined into one self-contained HTML), reads graph.json. Features: expand/collapse compound nodes (arbitrary depth), splittable meta-edges with counts (collapsedEdges), focus/neighborhood (highlightNeighbors), pan/zoom. No server, no Java. Publishable via Artifact or opened locally. Start from the expand-collapse repo demo.html (~hundreds of lines).
close_reason: Viewer shipped: devtools/archviz/ vendors pinned cytoscape(3.30.2)/layout-base(2.0.1)/cose-base(2.2.0)/fcose(2.2.0) + viewer.js engine + index.html.tmpl. write_viewer() assembles a fully-inlined static index.html that fetches ./graph.json; --json emits graph.json + index.html. Verified: self-contained (0 external script src), wheel bundles all 6 assets, cardiac assembles (66KB json + 715KB html). 74 devtools tests, ruff/graph/ast-grep clean. Engine = the user-confirmed m5c.1 spike (manual aggregation, drill, focus-isolate).

description: archmap emits graph.json: nodes = modules (folder≡package≡module, from packages), edges = contains (nesting DAG) + imports (deps) with counts. This committed json IS the diffable erosion signal (replaces the mermaid picture-diff). Stable key ordering for clean diffs. Reuses the grimp extraction already in graph.py/archmap.py. Class-shape + mirror test.
close_reason: graph.json emitter done: Archmap.graph_data() -> {nodes:[{id,label,parent,descendants}], edges:[{source,target,weight}]} (weight = grimp import-detail count); write_json() deterministic (sorted); --json CLI flag. 16 unit tests green (containment parent, descendant count, weighted edges, deterministic write, --json). title: spike: self-contained cytoscape viewer on cardiac graph -> Artifact, judge + decide mermaid fate
description: PREREQ. Emit graph.json from cardiac's grimp graph, build ONE self-contained cytoscape.js HTML (expand-collapse + fcose + focus), publish via the Artifact tool. Judge live on the real codebase: R1 arbitrary-depth collapse, R2 splittable meta-edge WITH count [7] (cytoscape's collapsedEdges), R3 focus/neighborhood highlight, R4 compound layout readable. DECIDE from what you see: (a) is the viewer good enough to build for real, (b) mermaid's fate — drop entirely vs keep one shallow inline figure. Throwaway output + decision, no shipped code.
close_reason: SPIKE DONE — viewer works, user confirmed. Self-contained cytoscape HTML on cardiac (105 nodes/417 edges): manual aggregation engine (dropped cytoscape-expand-collapse — its grouping can't aggregate to package-level after collapseAll). Verified headless: collapsed view = one counted arrow per pair (cardioseg->core[137], cardioview->core[24], sums exact). R1 arbitrary-depth drill (click box=drill one level, click open pkg=fold), R2 splittable counted arrows, R3 focus=isolate neighbourhood+zoom, fcose layout+cose fallback. Reference: scratchpad/build_viewer.py. DECISIONS: (1) BUILD for real; (2) mermaid DROPPED entirely; (3) delivery = graph.json + static index.html viewer -> GitHub Pages per repo.

title: archviz v2: interactive cytoscape architecture explorer (supersedes 2vt mermaid)
description: Pivot the archmap output from a committed static mermaid mirror-tree (shipped v1.5.0 / epic 2vt) to an INTERACTIVE explorer. Frontier decided across research (research/deep_dives/2026-07-16_interactive_archviz_libs.md): no tool does auto-derived + diffable + interactive at once (interactivity needs a runtime, a commit is static), so the pattern is: commit grimp's graph.json (the diffable erosion signal) + ship one self-contained cytoscape.js HTML viewer that hydrates it (expand/collapse arbitrary depth, splittable meta-edges with counts, focus/neighborhood, fcose compound layout) — no server (dodges tach --web remote upload), no Java (dodges Structurizr). Rip out the redundant deep mermaid mirror-tree + --check gate + pre-commit regen; keep AT MOST one shallow top-tier mermaid figure for a zero-dependency inline README glance (or drop entirely — a values call in .4). Edge types in scope: contains (nesting) + imports (deps). Inheritance/call-graph OUT (call graph is statically approximate; revisit only with full typing). Stays in sdlc-devtools; extract to a standalone python-autoarch only once real + a second consumer (not speculative).
close_reason: EPIC DONE — archviz v2 shipped v1.6.0. grimp -> committed graph.json (diff-truth) + self-contained interactive cytoscape viewer (manual aggregation: folded pair = one counted arrow; fold/expand any depth; focus-isolate). Vendored pinned libs in devtools/archviz/. Wired nox/CI/pre-commit + opt-in GitHub Pages (archviz_pages). Mermaid fully dropped. All 6 children closed. Spike->emitter->viewer->rip-mermaid->wiring->e2e+release.

Write to a docs/architecture/ mirror tree. Advisory doc-gen (exit 0). Follows the class-shape rule (helpers = methods, thin main) + carries a per-engine mirror test. Depends on the spike decision.

Archmap engine class (all helpers methods, thin main). 9 unit tests (stub-graph aggregation + real-grimp integration). Green: ruff, ast-grep class-shape, graph --assert test-mirror, deptry, 66 pytest. Proven on cardiac-seg (17 docs). No version bump (bump at epic release).

title: archmap spike: engine choice + feasibility

title: remove the ratchet: gates are legislated scaffold config, not freeze-current
description: The ratchet (auto-measure current metric + freeze it as ceiling + escape-hatch bump) is the wrong mechanism: seeding a ceiling from current state ratifies the mess (e.g. complexity=16 seeded from our worst function). Correct model = scaffold ships an OPINIONATED LEGISLATED default, tunable per-repo via config when needed (what copier config is for); knobs added on real need, not speculatively. Rip out: Ratchet primitive (_common) + tests; [tool.magic_literals]/[tool.complexity] ceilings; --max-* flags; freeze-current language. magic_literals -> ADVISORY (no universal number: 0 too strict, N arbitrary). complexity -> ADVISORY (ruff C901=10 stays THE fixed gate; radon just reports). Structure thresholds/vulture/coverage untouched (already legislated default+slot). v1.4.0.

title: v1.0 regressed lint_paths/jscpd_paths/data_env_var to when:false -> copier UPDATE cannot preserve non-default FACTS (adoption loss)
description:
Found adopting v1.0 into cardiac-seg (systole). v1.0 flipped three per-repo FACT vars to when:false (computed from packages): lint_paths, jscpd_paths, data_env_var. data_env_var was a when:true ANSWER in v0.17 (added by skr GAP3a precisely so a repo reading CARDIAC_DATA could override the CARDIOSEG_DATA default); v1.0 reverted it. lint_paths + jscpd_paths were also when:true answers in v0.17.

IMPACT on the UPDATE path (not fresh gen): copier update does NOT honor these for a consumer whose value differs from the packages-derived default. Empirically on cardiac (3 runs, v0.17->v1.0):
- copier update --defaults (existing .copier-answers.yml still holds lint_paths=core cardioseg cardioview tests, jscpd_paths=core cardioseg cardioview/web/src, data_env_var=CARDIAC_DATA): the rendered ci.yml recomputes from packages -> enforced lint scans only 'core cardioseg' (drops cardioview+tests), jscpd scans 'core cardioseg' (drops cardioview/web/src), tests env sets CARDIOSEG_DATA. The three keys are also STRIPPED from the regenerated .copier-answers.yml (when:false vars are not persisted).
- copier update --defaults --data lint_paths=... --data jscpd_paths=... --data data_env_var=CARDIAC_DATA: --data is IGNORED for when:false vars on the update path (same wrong render).
- CONTROL: a fresh copier COPY --defaults --data <same three> renders all three CORRECTLY (core cardioseg cardioview tests / cardioview/web/src / CARDIAC_DATA). So the override mechanism works on copy, only the UPDATE path drops it.

NET for a consumer: adopting v1.0 via copier update LOSES per-repo FACTS. For cardiac specifically: (a) enforced-lint scope shrinks to packages (cardioview + tests no longer linted), (b) jscpd DRY scope drops cardioview/web/src, (c) tests job sets CARDIOSEG_DATA but cardiac code reads CARDIAC_DATA -> data-gated CI tests ERROR (env unset) instead of skipping. (c) has NO LOCAL-SLOT workaround: the tests env: block is template-owned with no slot, and it precedes the base pytest step, so a consumer cannot inject CARDIAC_DATA. This blocks a no-loss v1.0 adoption for cardiac-seg (owner mandate: no losing stuff vs main).

RECOMMEND: restore lint_paths, jscpd_paths, data_env_var to when:true (asked, persisted in .copier-answers.yml) so their values survive copier update — reinstating skr GAP3a's design (data_env_var as an answer) and extending it to the two path vars. Keeping the packages-derived value as the DEFAULT is fine; the regression is making them non-overridable on update. Alternatively, if they must stay computed, honor an explicit .copier-answers.yml / --data override on the update path. VERIFY: a repo with lint_paths != packages does a copier update and the wider scope survives in the rendered ci.yml + persists in .copier-answers.yml.

Origin: cardiac-seg v1.0 adoption (bd cardiac-seg-zjaf / 44o4).
close_reason: FIXED v1.0.2. lint_paths/jscpd_paths/data_env_var made asked (when:true) so a non-default value persists in .copier-answers.yml + survives copier update (a when:false computed var is neither stored nor honored on the update path). data_env_var gated when=enable_ml and MOVED below enable_ml in copier.yml — copier evaluates vars top-to-bottom so a when-expr only sees vars above it; that ordering was why it wasn't persisting. VERIFIED: new e2e test_copier_update_preserves_nondefault_fact_vars generates with lint_paths='core viewer tests' + data_env_var=CUSTOM_DATA, runs copier update, asserts both survive in the rendered ci.yml AND persist in .copier-answers.yml (+ the portable rule still flows in). 96 tests green. NOTE for consumers: cardiac's current .copier-answers.yml holds only packages (the FACTS were already stripped by the v1.0 update) — cardiac must re-set lint_paths/data_env_var once on its next update via --data or the answers file; thereafter they persist.

title: archviz decision gate: does compaction meet the ranked reqs, or is ELK warranted?
notes: DECISION INPUT (Stage A shipped, commit 2205c41). Measured vs fcose floor: under-filled 25→0 in EVERY view, median fill 0.15→0.37 at all/all, canvas 7649x9670→4803x4902, deterministic (reseed dropped), magic constants retired. Compression #1 / determinism #3 / no-magic #5 all met; structure #2 preserved (well-filled clusters untouched). NOT met: planarity #4 (crossings ROSE 3183→5562 — compression↔planarity tension) and up-down #6 (compaction grids under-filled boxes in reading order = adjacency, not flow). THE GATE: is Stage A good enough (guaranteed compression+determinism, zero new deps, working viewer kept) or does the crossing count + the want for #4/#6 justify ELK's 1.6MB + full engine swap (Stage B, 42b.4)? Needs owner visual confirmation on docs/architecture/index.html. Per owner: 'ill tell you if its successful.'
close_reason: GATE DECIDED: ship Stage A, defer ELK. Compression #1 / determinism #3 / no-magic #5 met and owner-confirmed working. ELK buys planarity #4 + up-down #6 (both below the met priorities) for 1.6MB + full-engine-swap risk — not warranted now. Stage B (42b.4) + layered (a7x.2) deferred behind this gate; reopen if planarity/up-down starts mattering.

title: archviz Stage A: deterministic compaction on fcose (extend parking to recapture outliers)
notes: LOWEST-RISK fix for the top requirement (compression), no new dependency, keeps the working viewer. The parking pass already grids EDGELESS children under the cluster; extend it to also pull in any GEOMETRIC OUTLIER — a child far from its siblings' centroid relative to their spread — into the tidy block, even if it has an edge (an empty box is worse than one re-routed arrow). Threshold DERIVED from sibling spread (median + MAD), not a tuned constant (req #5). GATE (harness): zero pathological boxes (no full-width/height empty strips), median fill materially above the fcose baseline, determinism holds. Also REMOVE the interim magic constants (edgeElasticity/gravityCompound/cross-module tagging) — the compaction makes them unnecessary. Does NOT deliver planarity(#4)/up-down(#6); that is the decision gate's job.
close_reason: Unified force pass + deterministic compaction into shared layout.js (viewer + harness both import it); compaction (in-box-degree parking, then reading-order regrid of any under-filled box via the harness's derived ratio) guarantees compression; retired the two interim magic knobs; dropped the viewer reseed for determinism. Result: under-filled 25→0 all-views, median fill 0.15→0.37 at all/all, canvas ~halved, deterministic. Cost: crossings 3183→5562 (compression↔planarity tension) — decision-gate input.

title: archviz: Node layout-measurement harness (fill %, crossings, determinism)
notes: PRECONDITION for every layout stage — turns 'makes no sense' into a number. A Node script loads sdlc-devtools/docs/architecture/graph.json, runs a candidate layout (fcose via headless cytoscape or emitted positions; ELK via elkjs directly), and reports: per-compound fill (node+routing area / box area), count of under-filled boxes, total edge crossings, canvas size, and a determinism check (two runs -> identical positions). Every layout change is scored here BEFORE a browser look. Prototype already exists in scratchpad (elk fill measurement); productionise it under sdlc-devtools/ (a python -m or a small node harness committed with a fixed graph.json fixture). Establish the fcose BASELINE numbers first so later stages have a floor to beat.
close_reason: Node harness (measure.cjs) scores fill/crossings/determinism/canvas headless on the pinned fixture; baseline recorded; drift-guard holds the shipped-layout copy to viewer.js; determinism shown achievable via randomize:false

title: (EPIC) archviz layout: principled, deterministic, compressed — retire the force-tuning guesswork
notes:
GOAL: the architecture viewer's auto-layout satisfies the ranked requirements in docs/archviz_layout_requirements.md — compression first, then meaningful position, determinism, planarity, no magic constants, up-down OK, keep the compound-map + all UI. 'Successful' is measured by a Node harness (fill %, crossing count, determinism) against the real graph.json, with the owner confirming the final visual.

BASELINE (now): cytoscape-fcose + a hand parking pass. Force layout cannot guarantee a tight compound box — one edge flings a child, the box balloons empty. Current committed state carries interim magic-constant tweaks (cross-module edge weakening, gravityCompound 8 / gravityRangeCompound 5): strictly better than untuned, but exactly the magic numbers req #5 bans. They are the baseline to beat, not the target.

APPROACH IS STAGE-GATED, not decided up front. Stage A (deterministic compaction on fcose) is the proportionate fix for the actual complaint (compression) at lowest risk. Stage B (ELK layered engine) is only worth its 1.6MB + full-engine-swap cost IF planarity/up-down prove to matter after A. Do NOT swap a working viewer for a layout nit without that evidence.

PROTOTYPE DATA (ELK, node, real graph.json): module boxes 80-87% filled (fixes the empty-box complaint); class boxes spacious (layered routing); canvas ~7600x6250 even at min spacing; per-compound layoutOptions required (root-only options do not propagate). elkjs is 1.6MB inlined -> page 0.7MB -> ~2.3MB.

CONSTRAINT: content + controls are DONE and good; layout work touches placement/sizing/order/compression ONLY. Do not regress the working interactivity.
close_reason: Stage A shipped (v1.26.0, PR #33 merged): harness + deterministic shelf compaction, owner-confirmed working. Gate 42b.3 decided (defer ELK); 42b.4 remains as a deferred bead if planarity/up-down ever warrants it.

title: group the engines by co-use, with the import graph as evidence
description:
The engine half of bd yfv. UNBLOCKED -- bd 72g ruled the module path is ours, so engines may move.

THE RULE: "fire together wire together". A folder records modules that are USED together. Do not group by
kind: `analysis/` holding twenty engines is one flat directory with an extra path segment, and grouping
modules by KIND is the mistake CLAUDE.md bans for types.

EVIDENCE FIRST: `python -m devtools.archmap devtools` draws the import graph the package already has. Read
it before naming a single folder. If a proposed group is not a cluster there, it is a taste grouping and
does not ship. Candidate seams worth CHECKING against the graph rather than assuming -- the arrow producers
(arrows/calls/classes) versus the gates that consume their edges (contracts/composition/envy/demeter)
versus the file/line-level readers that share nothing but `trees`. That is a hypothesis, not a plan.

MECHANICS, once the groups are real:
  - the runner keeps BARE names: `Batch.engine_class` discovers modules across subpackages
    (pkgutil.walk_packages) instead of resolving `devtools.{name}`. `--gate graph,demeter` unchanged.
  - direct `python -m devtools.<tool>` paths move. Every site is copier-rendered (ci.yml.jinja,
    .pre-commit-config.yaml.jinja, noxfile.py.jinja), so consumers get them on `copier update`.
  - the mirror convention follows the path, so tests/unit/devtools/<sub>/<name>.py happens by itself and
    BOTH mirror gates verify it did. That is the cheapest possible check on a big move.
  - UPGRADING.md entry + version bump. Sequence with bd 6s6 so consumers coordinate once, not twice.

DO NOT start here. The plumbing half (bd 2wt) is the same principle with no contract to negotiate; do that
first and let it prove the mirror-follows-path claim on eight modules before betting twenty on it.
notes:
IMPORT-GRAPH CHECKPOINT (the owner asked to see this BEFORE grouping). Analyzed both co-use signals:

(1) INTER-ENGINE IMPORTS (who imports whom) — SPARSE. Only edges:
    arrows  <- archmap, composition, contracts, graph   (fan-in 4)
    calls   <- archmap, contracts, graph                 (fan-in 3)
    classes <- archmap, envy, graph                      (fan-in 3)
    config  <- astgrep                                    (1)
  EVERY other analyzer imports NO sibling engine. They wire to PLUMBING, not to each other.

(2) PLUMBING-FINGERPRINT clustering (which machinery each shares) — mostly SINGLETONS/PAIRS. Largest
    cluster is 3 ({cli,trees}: data_clumps,lcom,magic_literals); everything else is 1-2. No natural
    3-4 folder partition exists.

FINDING: the import graph does NOT support grouping the ~20 analyzers into co-use folders. Any
partition would be by KIND (analysis/ quality/ structure/) = the flat-dir-with-an-extra-segment
bucket the bead + CLAUDE.md forbid. The flat analyzer layout is what the graph says is TRUE.

The ONLY tree-derived sub-structure with evidence: arrows/calls/classes are a shared-PRIMITIVES hub
(the AST arrow/call-edge/class-index builders other engines consume). A 'primitives/' subpackage is
defensible; even it is thin (3 modules).

RECOMMENDATION (owner to decide): shrink yfv.2 from 'group twenty engines into folders' to EITHER
(a) extract only arrows/calls/classes into devtools/primitives/ and leave the rest flat, OR (b) keep
analyzers flat entirely and close yfv.2 as 'the graph shows no grouping to make'. Do NOT force
kind-buckets. Also: the ENGINE move renames python -m devtools.<tool> (72g decided: engines move,
consumers re-render via copier update) AND requires fixing Cli.prog, which today is
'python -m devtools.{file-stem}' and omits any subpackage segment (flagged in 2wt close).
close_reason: Grouped by co-use with the import graph as evidence (the checkpoint the owner asked for). The graph showed the analyzers wire to plumbing, not each other; the ONE fan-in cluster is arrows/calls/classes (the shared structural read-models: class index, call arrows, dep arrows). Extracted those into devtools/primitives/; left the ~17 leaf analyzers flat because grouping them would be by KIND (forbidden). Owner steer was 'extract more core, apply good judgement' — this is the core the graph supports, not a bucket. Consumer-visible (v1.25.0): python -m devtools.arrows|calls -> devtools.primitives.arrows|calls, re-rendered via copier across noxfile/CI/pre-commit (72g); classes has no CLI so only two rename. Also fixed Cli.tool to resolve the subpackage segment (the prog quirk flagged in 2wt) and switched engine discovery to walk_packages so subpackage engines aren't dropped. 567 pass, nox lint green; e2e running.

title: derive UNDRIVEABLE and BESPOKE from the code, not from literals
description:
The capability half of bd yfv. Two hardcoded sets say what a module CAN do; the code already knows.

run.py            UNDRIVEABLE = {config, archmap, analytics, run} -- "cannot be constructed as Engine(pkgs)"
engine_interface  BESPOKE     = {config, archmap, run}            -- "exempt from the report()/run_assert()
                                contract" (a DIFFERENT question, deliberately -- see the comment there)

THE PRECEDENT IS IN THE SAME FILE, twice:
  - `Batch.build` routes by inspecting __init__'s signature ("an engine that resolves names declares
    `resolver`, one that only walks the source declares `trees`") rather than by a per-engine list
  - `Batch.engine_class` was moved in PR #30 off "the alphabetically first class defined here" onto "the
    class answering report()" -- because the former was a coincidence that broke the moment a value object
    sorted before its engine

"Can this be constructed as Engine(packages)?" is the same shape of question and wants the same answer:
inspect it. `analytics` is the case that proves the sets are not the same question -- it honours the
report()/run_assert() contract perfectly (so it is NOT bespoke) but takes __init__(repo, areas) (so it IS
undriveable). A derivation must keep them distinct; collapsing them silently dropped analytics from the
contract test once already, and run.py's comment records that.

WATCH FOR: a module with no engine class at all (`config` is close to this), and the ordering hazard the
engine_class fix already hit. Derive, then assert the derived sets equal today's literals BEFORE deleting
them -- if they differ, one of the two is wrong and that is the actual finding.
close_reason: Derived both capability sets, per the bead's derive-then-assert method. FINDING: the two sets are NOT the same question and only one had a consumer. BESPOKE (contract-exempt, consumed by the interface test) derives cleanly as 'the class the runner would drive answers no report()' = {archmap,config,run}, matching the literal exactly; analytics correctly stays OUT (real report(), just an unconstructable __init__). Replaced the literal with the derivation + a guard test pinning the derived set to the known three (a 4th contractless module now fails loud, not skips). UNDRIVEABLE/NOT_ENGINES in run.py were DEAD (consumed by nothing) AND self-inconsistent: the comment says 'cannot be constructed as Engine(packages)' but the set listed archmap+run, which DO take packages — matching only under the different predicate 'cannot be DRIVEN by the batch runner'. Deleted rather than derive-into-dead-code; if the taxonomy doc is wanted back, re-add with the corrected predicate + a consumer. 566 pass, nox lint green.

title: (EPIC) the tree is the authority: devtools structure comes from folders
description:
GOAL: devtools' organization is READ FROM THE TREE, not declared in string sets. When the two can disagree,
the tree wins because the tree is what runs.

THE PRINCIPLE (the owner's, from the PR #30 review, verbatim because everything else follows):

    "the module hierarchy comes from the folder structure, not from hardcoded manual lists of
     organizational placement"

and on what the folders should be:

    "the grouping should be obvious to someone that understands devtools. fire together wire together"

FIRE TOGETHER WIRE TOGETHER is the design rule, and it is a real constraint, not a slogan: a folder records
modules that are USED together, not modules that are ALIKE. `analysis/` holding twenty engines because they
are all analyzers is filing by KIND -- one flat directory with an extra path segment, and the same mistake
CLAUDE.md bans for types. The import graph already knows which modules co-fire; `python -m devtools.archmap
devtools` draws it. Folders RECORD that layering, they do not impose one.

THREE KINDS OF LIST, and only the first is what the principle governs. Sorting them is the first work, since
"delete the lists" would take policy and capability with it:

  ORGANIZATIONAL -- says WHERE a module belongs. Becomes a folder; the set is computed by walking it.
      run.py            PLUMBING = {_common, names, trees, pyproject, resolve, omit, cli, layout}
      engine_interface  BESPOKE  = {config, archmap, run}
  CAPABILITY -- says WHAT a module can do. Derive from the CODE. The precedent is in the same file:
      run.py            UNDRIVEABLE -- and `Batch.build` ALREADY routes by inspecting __init__'s signature,
                        and `Batch.engine_class` moved off "alphabetically first class" onto "the class
                        answering report()" in PR #30. Same move, one more time.
  POLICY -- says what a REPO enforces. Stays a list, do not touch.
      noxfile           --gate {...} / --report {...}. run.py's docstring is explicit: "MECHANISM, NOT
                        POLICY. Nothing here decides what a project enforces."

WHY IT IS CHEAPER THAN IT LOOKS: the mirror convention follows the PATH. layout.py builds
tests/unit/<pkg>/<module.parent>/<name>.py, so moving devtools/foo.py to devtools/sub/foo.py moves its
mirror to tests/unit/devtools/sub/foo.py with no gate change and no config. The test tree reorganises itself
-- and both mirror gates verify it did.

RULED ALREADY (bd 72g, closed): the module path is OURS. Engines move; the rename ships consumer-visible on
a version bump. Every invocation site is copier-rendered, so a consumer gets the new paths on `copier
update` -- a template re-render, not a hand-migration. The runner keeps BARE names by discovering modules
across subpackages, so `--gate graph,demeter` never learns about folders.

END STATE, all of it or the goal is not met:
  - sdlc-devtools/tests is LINTED (it is currently linted by nothing, and ~260 tests just landed there)
  - PLUMBING and BESPOKE are gone as literals; the folders answer those questions
  - UNDRIVEABLE is derived from the constructor signature, not listed
  - engines are grouped by co-use, with the import graph as the evidence, and no folder is a kind-bucket
  - `python -m devtools.run --gate <bare names>` is unchanged
  - the template's rendered runners carry the new paths; UPGRADING.md tells consumers what moved
  - every gate still green, and the mirror gates confirm the test tree followed
close_reason: All 5 children done. The tree is now the authority: plumbing/ (machinery, bd 2wt) + primitives/ (shared read-models, bd yfv.2) are folders whose membership is WALKED, not listed; the contract-exempt set is DERIVED from the code (yfv.1); the scaffold's own tests are linted and single-sourced (a19, 1gj). Shipping as v1.25.0. The only kind-bucket temptation (grouping the 20 leaf analyzers) was refused on import-graph evidence — they wire to plumbing, not each other, so flat is what's true.

title: mirror gate: the override exemption rests on a presumption nothing checks
description:
FOUND BY MEASURING IT during PR #30 review, after asserting the opposite in a report.

devtools/mirror.py exempts a method that a same-module BASE also declares, on the grounds that it is one
polymorphic contract covered by the base's test. Nothing verifies that. The rule PRESUMES the base's test
exercises the subclasses.

MEASURED, whole package: 6 methods are exempted by it, and every one is in devtools/layout.py --
_Mirror/_Off .mirror_of and .missing (and the two that were _Flat before it was removed). So the exemption
exists entirely to spare ONE module: the one written in the same session that added the rule. That is the
same shape as the "private class" exemption that was removed in the same PR -- friction in one file
generalised into a rule.

It happens to be TRUE here only because test_mirror_of was written as a table over every layout value. Had
it tested one value, the other strategy's override would have been untested and the gate would have said
nothing.

THE ARGUMENT AGAINST THE EXEMPTION is the convention itself: these are different implementations with
different behaviour -- that is what a strategy IS. `_Off.mirror_of` returns None; `_Mirror.mirror_of`
computes a path. Under rule 1 each is a public member wanting its own named test.

IF IT IS DROPPED, no new machinery is needed and the naming already works:
  - the abstract base falls out on its own -- TestLayout.mirror_of raises NotImplementedError, so
    `is_declaration` already exempts it
  - the ambiguity rule then demands test_mirror_mirror_of / test_off_mirror_of and the two `missing`
    counterparts, and the message names each exact function
  - cost: ~4 tests, one file, pinning behaviours that currently rest on how one table happened to be written

ALTERNATIVE, if the exemption is kept: it must be documented as a PRESUMPTION rather than as a fact, since
its docstring currently states the coverage as though it were checked.

Do not decide this by whether layout.py is inconvenienced.
notes: DECIDED by the owner 2026-07-21: DROP the exemption. Each strategy override gets its own named test. The abstract base falls out via is_declaration and the ambiguity rule names the replacements, so no new machinery -- roughly four tests in layout.py.

title: is `python -m devtools.<tool>` a public contract? (blocks the engine half of 2wt)
description:
A RULING IS NEEDED, not an investigation. bd 2wt says the module hierarchy comes from folders; the plumbing
half can move today, and the ENGINE half is blocked on this one question.

THE FACT: every engine is invoked as `python -m devtools.<tool>`, and that path is wired into
  - template/noxfile.py.jinja, template/.pre-commit-config.yaml.jinja, template/.github/workflows/ci.yml.jinja
  - sdlc-devtools/noxfile.py (the package's own self-gate)
  - three consumer repos, which inherit all of the above on `copier update`
  - devtools/run.py -- Batch.engine_class resolves `devtools.{name}` from a bare name, and `--gate graph,demeter`
    is a comma-separated list of those bare names
  - every engine docstring's "Run:" line, and docs/RULE_INVENTORY.md

Moving `purity.py` to `<group>/purity.py` renames the invocation everywhere in that list.

THE THREE OPTIONS, and the third is the trap:

  A. THE PATH IS PUBLIC -- engines stay flat, only plumbing gets a folder. The package then has exactly one
     level of hierarchy and 20-odd engine peers at the top, which is honest if the engines really are peers.
     Cheapest, and it is what 2wt's first move assumes.

  B. THE PATH IS OURS -- engines move into folders, `--gate` learns dotted names, and the rename ships as a
     consumer-visible change on a version bump, exactly like the test_layout collapse in v1.24.0. Costs an
     UPGRADING entry and a coordinated bump. Defensible: the scaffold has changed consumer-visible contracts
     before when the old one was wrong.

  C. FLAT SHIMS -- `devtools/purity.py` re-exporting main() from `devtools/<group>/purity.py`. Preserves the
     path and is the WRONG answer: it is 20 files of duplication whose only job is to hide the structure the
     folders were added to express, and it leaves two import paths for one engine. Recorded so it is
     rejected on purpose rather than discovered halfway through.

DECIDE A OR B FIRST. Then bd 2wt's engine half is mechanical either way, because the mirror convention
follows the path -- moving a module moves its test with no gate change (devtools/layout.py builds
tests/unit/<pkg>/<module.parent>/<name>.py).

NOTE the asymmetry that makes A more attractive than it looks: `run.py`'s PLUMBING is organizational and
must become a folder under 2wt regardless of this ruling, and none of those modules has a CLI. So the
principle can be satisfied in full for the half that has no contract, and this ruling only governs whether
it extends to the half that does.
close_reason:
RULED: B -- the module path is OURS. Engines move into folders; the rename ships as a consumer-visible
change on a version bump. Delegated to me ("i have no idea, ive let you in charge of that").

THE COST WAS OVERSTATED IN THIS BEAD, and checking it is what decided the ruling. Every site that invokes
`python -m devtools.<tool>` is COPIER-RENDERED -- template/.github/workflows/ci.yml.jinja,
template/.pre-commit-config.yaml.jinja, template/noxfile.py.jinja. A consumer running `copier update` gets
the new paths for free. This is a template re-render, not a hand-migration across three repos. The original
text listed those files as if a human had to edit each one.

THE RUNNER KEEPS BARE NAMES, and this is not the rejected shim. `Batch.engine_class` resolves
`devtools.{name}` from a bare name today; it can instead discover modules across subpackages
(pkgutil.walk_packages, the same walk engine_interface already does). One implementation, no duplicate
module, no second import path -- so `--gate graph,demeter,purity` is unchanged and the noxfile's gate list
never learns about folders. Only the DIRECT `python -m devtools.<tool>` module path moves, and every one of
those lives in a rendered file.

WHY NOT A: the owner's grouping principle is "obvious to someone who understands devtools -- fire together
wire together". Under A the grouping reaches 8 plumbing modules and stops, leaving ~20 engine peers flat.
A principle that covers a quarter of the package is not the principle.

PRECEDENT, from the release this came out of: v1.24.0 changed what `test_layout = "mirror"` MEANS, removed
`flat` outright, and flipped a default -- all consumer-visible, all because the old shape was wrong. The
standing rule is that the scaffold's job ends at the scaffold and consumers' agents decide adoption; it is
not that consumer-visible contracts never change.

SEQUENCE IT WITH bd 6s6. Consumers are already converting to the method-mirror convention. Landing the path
change in the same bump is one coordination instead of two -- the same argument that flipped the test_layout
default rather than sparing them the rename.

title: devtools: the module hierarchy comes from folders, not hardcoded lists
description:
DECIDED, from PR #30 review -- not an open question:

    THE MODULE HIERARCHY COMES FROM THE FOLDER STRUCTURE, NOT FROM HARDCODED MANUAL LISTS OF
    ORGANIZATIONAL PLACEMENT.

devtools is 31 flat modules and its organization is spelled as string sets in code. The tree is the
authority; a list that says where things belong is a second authority that can disagree with it, and
disagreeing silently is the failure mode -- PLUMBING was hand-edited for `layout` during PR #30, and
forgetting it would have made the interface test demand report()/run_assert() of a config strategy.

THREE KINDS OF LIST, and only the first kind is what the principle is about. Sorting them is the first
piece of work, because "delete all the lists" would take policy and capability with it:

  ORGANIZATIONAL -- says WHERE a module belongs. Must become a folder.
      run.py            PLUMBING = {_common, names, trees, pyproject, resolve, omit, cli, layout}
      engine_interface  BESPOKE  = {config, archmap, run}
    These name a group. The group should be a directory, and the set should be computed by walking it.

  CAPABILITY -- says WHAT a module can do. Derive from the CODE, not from a folder and not from a list.
      run.py            UNDRIVEABLE = {config, archmap, analytics, run}  ("cannot be built as Engine(pkgs)")
    THE PRECEDENT IS IN THE SAME FILE: `Batch.build` already routes by inspecting __init__'s signature
    ("routed by SIGNATURE ... so sharing costs no per-engine branching"). Whether a class can be constructed
    as Engine(packages) is the same question asked the same way. `Batch.engine_class` was likewise moved off
    "the alphabetically first class" onto "the class answering report()" in this PR -- same move.

  POLICY -- says what a REPO enforces. Legitimately config, stays a list, do not touch.
      noxfile           --gate {...} / --report {...}
    run.py's docstring is explicit: "MECHANISM, NOT POLICY ... Nothing here decides what a project
    enforces." A repo choosing which engines block is not the package's hierarchy.

FIRST MOVE, contained and with zero consumer impact: give the eight plumbing modules a subpackage and
compute PLUMBING by walking it. Nothing invokes `python -m devtools.resolve`, so nothing outside moves.
That single change turns a hand-kept set into a fact about the tree and is the whole principle in miniature.

THE ONE DECISION NEEDED BEFORE THE ENGINES MOVE: `python -m devtools.<tool>` is a documented contract,
pinned in three consumer repos' noxfiles / pre-commit hooks / CI workflows. Grouping engines into folders
renames every one of those invocations. That is a public-API decision, not a refactor -- and a flat shim
re-exporting main() to preserve the path is the duplication this repo refuses elsewhere. Ask before doing
it; it may want its own bead.

WHAT MAKES IT CHEAP: the mirror convention follows the PATH. layout.py builds
tests/unit/<pkg>/<module.parent>/<name>.py, so moving devtools/foo.py to devtools/sub/foo.py moves its
mirror to tests/unit/devtools/sub/foo.py with no gate change and no config. The test tree reorganises itself.

DO NOT let a folder become a bucket. `analysis/` holding twenty engines is one flat directory with an extra
path segment, and grouping modules by KIND is the same mistake CLAUDE.md bans for types. Read the import
graph first (`python -m devtools.archmap devtools`): the directories should RECORD a layering the code
already has, not impose one it does not.
notes:
THE DEPENDENCY ON bd 72g IS PARTIAL, and beads records it as whole-issue. Read it as: the ENGINE half waits
on that ruling. The PLUMBING half is unblocked and can be done today -- those eight modules have no CLI, so
no contract governs where they live, and moving them is the whole principle in miniature with zero consumer
impact. Do not let the link stall the half that is ready.
close_reason: Moved the 8 plumbing modules into devtools/plumbing/ (git mv, history preserved) + their 8 mirror tests into tests/unit/devtools/plumbing/. PLUMBING is now DERIVED by walking plumbing.__path__ in run.py, not a literal. The mirror gate accepted the whole reorganization with ZERO config change (layout builds the path from module.parent) — nox lint green, 565 pass. Interface-test filter now skips subpackages structurally via info.ispkg (the tree speaks); PLUMBING kept as belt-and-suspenders + shared vocabulary. NOTES for follow-ups: (1) PLUMBING could be deleted entirely in favour of pure info.ispkg — kept derived to honour the bead + seed yfv.1's UNDRIVEABLE. (2) Cli.prog = 'python -m devtools.{file-stem}' omits any subpackage segment; harmless now (no plumbing module has a main, all engines are top-level) but the ENGINE-move bead (yfv.2) MUST fix prog derivation to include the subpackage or it will advertise a wrong invocation.

title: consumers: adopt the method-mirror convention (one conversion, both renames)
description:
NOT OUR WORK. Filed so the scaffold side knows what it handed downstream, and so a ticket back has somewhere
to land. cardiac-seg / mindscape / synthscape agents decide whether and when -- they are the experts on
whether the convention fits their tree.

WHAT LANDS ON THEM at v1.24.0, on their next copier update:
  - devtools.mirror --assert BLOCKS: every public method needs a `test_<method>` that calls it and asserts.
    They will each open with a large finding count. On devtools it was 119 across 27 modules.
  - devtools.small --assert BLOCKS: no external data root / network / sleep / unseeded RNG in tests/unit.
    Measured 0 on all three trees during the ar6 scoping, so this one should be free.
  - test_layout default flips to "bare": every mirror test file drops its `test_` prefix, and the template
    now ships python_files = ["*.py"] + python_classes = [] alongside it.

ONE PASS, NOT TWO -- that is the whole reason the default flipped. The prefix rename is mechanical (one
`git mv` per file) and trivial next to the conversion; doing them separately would mean converting a suite
to a spelling the scaffold itself rejected, and the two would stay different forever.

ORDER THAT WORKED HERE (docs/UPGRADING.md has it in full):
  1. rename first -- mechanical, keeps the restructure diff readable
  2. take the "rename it" findings next: a test already calls and asserts, under the wrong name. 60 of our
     119 were this.
  3. "reached by N other modules" -> write the test. 44 of 119.
  4. "called only inside its own file" -> a visibility decision, usually an underscore. 15 of 119, and every
     one of ours turned out to be worth a test instead.

ESCAPES, both honest and both temporary: test_layout = "off" disables BOTH mirror gates while they work the
list down; test_layout = "mirror" keeps the old filenames but does NOT spare them the method gate, which is
the actual work. Per-method: `# devtools-ignore: test-mirror`. We added ZERO ignores converting devtools --
the residue the hatch was filed for did not materialise, which is worth them knowing before they reach for it.

The convention and the case AGAINST it are in docs/UNIT_TESTS.md. If it does not fit a tree, the ticket back
is a general-architecture question for us, not a local workaround down there.

title: convert sdlc-devtools' unit suite to the convention + write the doctrine
description:
The bulk of bd mjo. Converting our own unit suite is not a follow-up to the gates -- it is the work that
makes the gates real, and the only way they graduate to blocking against a tree that has actually been
through it.

MEASURED STARTING POINT: naming compliance is 0% and parametrization is 1%, so almost every test file
changes. 276 devtools unit tests, mean 1.4-1.8 asserts each, 190 with exactly one.

THE CONVERSION, per test module:
  - collapse the behaviour-named tests for one method into ONE test_<method> container
  - the cases become a PARAMETRIZED TABLE -- n combinations x m assertions
  - keep the behaviour description in the param `ids`. This is not a compromise: `test_definer[base-class-
    defines-it]` carries MORE information than either a behaviour-named function or a bare method name, and
    it is how the convention satisfies Google's "describe the behaviour" at case granularity while keeping
    the method-level container.
  - doubles: real -> fake -> stub, never a mock. Already settled practice here and merely unwritten --
    measured across all four repos, the mock library appears in 2 files / 6 occurrences versus dozens of
    hand-written _Fake* / _Stub* / _Boom. `_FakeBackbone(Backbone)` subclasses the real base, so it cannot
    drift from the contract; that is the pattern to follow.
  - fixtures produce INPUTS, never expected outputs. Where a domain invariant forces real construction,
    CALL production's constructor rather than reimplementing it. Carve-out: tests OF the construction path
    use literals, or they verify nothing.

PLR0915 IS AN ALLY HERE, not an obstacle -- a @parametrize table costs ~1 statement regardless of case
count, while longhand inline asserts hit the 50-statement ceiling. Do NOT carve it out of the tests/**
per-file-ignores. (It is absent from that carve-out today by omission rather than decision; the list was
written for asserts, magic numbers, bool flags, naming and fixture params.)

EXPECTED VALUES, by trust, as the conversion will have to choose one per case: hand-derived literal >
independent oracle (only if it shares no inputs or intermediates) > invariant/property > metamorphic
relation > recorded golden (regression only -- encodes current behaviour including bugs). Minimal Fixture
exists for DERIVABILITY, not speed: shrink the input until the oracle can be a literal.

STANDARD FIXTURE is permitted and is the owner's preference -- one canonical data story rather than a
bespoke fixture per test -- PROVIDED every test constructs its own instance. Standard Fixture (shared
DESIGN) is orthogonal to Shared Fixture (shared INSTANCE); only the second causes Erratic Tests. The form
is a builder whose defaults are the canonical example.

THE DOCTRINE, in docs/, linked from the root README. It must state the DISSENT rather than assert
consensus -- Google, Cooper and Beck argue the other way, and the doctrine says why traceability wins here.
A doctrine that pretends to be consensus loses its first argument with anyone who has read the SWE book.
Sourcing: research/deep_dives/2026-07-21_test_architecture_sota.md
notes:
DATA STORY for phase 3, settled 2026-07-21. Full detail in
research/deep_dives/2026-07-21_test_architecture_sota.md section 4b.

Owner's instinct: not a separate small fixture per test, but one or two canonical examples of how data
flows, usable at every level. That is Meszaros' STANDARD FIXTURE and it is a named pattern, not a smell.

The safety condition is a distinction people conflate:
    Standard Fixture   shared DESIGN    -- fine
    Shared Fixture     shared INSTANCE  -- causes Erratic Tests, violates Independent Test
    Fresh Fixture      per-test instance
They are orthogonal -- a Standard Fixture may be used as either. So one canonical story is legitimate
PROVIDED every test builds its own instance.

THE FORM: a builder whose DEFAULTS ARE THE CANONICAL EXAMPLE. One story to learn, fresh instance per test,
and only the varied axis visible in the test body. That also answers Minimal Fixture's real concern, which
is comprehension rather than byte count.

HARD RULE, and it is the guard against bd acq's oracle collapse:
    fixtures produce INPUTS, never EXPECTED OUTPUTS.
A builder that computes what production computes makes fixture and code agree by construction. Where
domain invariants force real construction, CALL production's constructor instead of reimplementing it
(consistent with the owner's "close to real objects unless pointless"). Carve-out: tests OF the
construction path use literals, or they verify nothing.

EXPECTED VALUES come from, by trust: hand-derived literal > independent oracle (only if it shares no
inputs/intermediates) > invariant/property > metamorphic relation > recorded golden (regression only --
encodes current behaviour including bugs). This is what Minimal Fixture is FOR: derivability, not speed.
Shrink the input until the oracle can be a literal. For ML the invariant and metamorphic rows are the
workhorses, and they also dissolve the reimplementation worry -- an invariant is not a second
implementation.

UNIT SCOPE ONLY. The per-joint contract and end-to-end invariant rows of the chain story are parked at the
epic.
close_reason:
The bulk, done -- devtools' unit suite converted and the doctrine written.

MEASURED: 119 mirror findings -> 0. Suite 306 -> 520 passing. Coverage 82% -> 97%, which clears even the
advisory 95% target that had never been met. Both new gates now BLOCK on the package that ships them.

Split into 7 parallel Opus agents over disjoint module sets, each verifying against the gate itself, with
docs/UNIT_TESTS.md plus the hand-written tests/unit/devtools/layout.py as the shared exemplar. Every agent
reported zero methods renamed to private and zero ignores added -- the residue the ignore list was filed for
did not materialise, which is worth knowing: the escape hatch ships unused.

Several conversions CORRECTED assumptions rather than encoding them, which is the conversion earning its
keep: assert_fitness on a clean 3-node path graph is not advisory-empty (the middle node is a chokepoint at
0.5); a custom test_root re-flags an already-satisfied tree with no fallback; annotation_names("list['Store']")
returns only {"list"}, so a TYPE_CHECKING type written that way produces no arrow.

docs/UNIT_TESTS.md states the convention, what enforces which rule, and -- section 5 -- the case AGAINST it:
Cooper's "a unit is a behaviour, not a method", the flattened pyramid, and structural coverage being the
cheap half of behavioural coverage. Recorded as a stated bet, not a proof.

title: phase 2: the ASSERT half + the per-method ignore
description:
PHASE 2 of bd mjo. Runs only if phase 1 (bd c6b) says the findings are real.

Adds the second half of the owner's rule 2: the test must not just CALL the method, it must ASSERT after
it -- on the returned value or the resulting state.

MEASURED COST, unit tests only, so this is a small phase by findings and a fiddly one by mechanism:
    sdlc-scaffold 0 | sdlc-devtools 0 | cardiac-seg 5 | mindscape 4 | synthscape 0    -> 9 total
devtools being at ZERO is what makes it the graduation candidate.

THREE MECHANISMS BEHIND THOSE 9, each needing different work:

  6  "DOES NOT RAISE" -- `tracking.Tracking.artifact("nonexistent.json")` with no assertion, because not
     throwing IS the assertion. Two honest resolutions and phase 2 must pick one per site:
       (a) the collaborator carries observable state, so there IS something to assert; or
       (b) the method goes on the per-method IGNORE list.
     Do NOT invent a third mechanism for this -- the ignore is the owner's and it already covers it.

  2  DELEGATED ASSERT -- the test calls a helper that asserts (`check_multiseed_split`). Needs transitive
     resolution through the call graph, which we have. NOTE this is also what test extraction produces, so
     without it the gate over-fires on exactly the refactor the complexity limits encourage.

  1  `raise AssertionError` inside a try, instead of `assert`. One extra AST node (ast.Raise).

THE IGNORE (owner's rule 4): per-method, RULE-NAMED, never blanket -- consistent with what the scaffold
already ships (`# ast-grep-ignore: py-dynamic-attr`, and the PGH003/PGH004 stance on blanket type-ignore
and noqa).

AND THE IGNORE LIST IS ITSELF THE SIGNAL. That is the enforceable form of the owner's claim 3: a method
that cannot be observed through a return value or state is suspect. A short list is fine. A GROWING list
is the smell. So this phase ships no separate "observability" machinery -- reviewing the list is the
mechanism.

GRADUATION: advisory, then blocking on devtools first (already at 0), then per-consumer at each repo's own
pace. Adoption downstream is their agents' call, not the scaffold's.
close_reason: absorbed into c6b -- the phase split is dropped per the goal, and a call check without the assert check is not the convention

title: (EPIC) move the unit test gates to the method-mirror convention
description:
GOAL: the scaffold's unit tests follow one stated convention, and gates enforce it.

Not a phased trial. The deliverable is the END STATE -- convention defined, gates built, sdlc-devtools'
own unit suite converted to it, gates blocking there. A convention nothing enforces is a preference, and
a gate that has never been run against a converted tree is a guess.

THE CONVENTION (the owner's design, recorded verbatim because everything else follows from it):

  1. class A -> test_A file; public method A.a -> test_a in that file
  2. that test CALLS A.a and ASSERTS after it -- on the returned value or the resulting state
  3. a method needing no assertion after the call is suspect
  4. a per-method IGNORE for the residue, rule-named, never blanket
  5. UNIT TESTS ONLY -- integration and e2e are out of scope and stay out (see the parked bead)
  6. test_<method> is a CONTAINER: doubles for setup, n parameter combinations x m assertions, so one test
     verifies n*m things about one method. Systematic and dense.
  7. verify STATE and RETURN VALUES, never call chains
  8. doubles stay CLOSE TO REAL: real -> fake -> stub, never a mock (the classicist position; note
     `unittest.mock` is named for the thing rule 7 excludes)
  9. fixtures produce INPUTS, never EXPECTED OUTPUTS -- otherwise fixture and code agree by construction,
     which is bd acq's oracle collapse arriving through the fixture
 10. a unit test is SMALL: no external data root, no network, no sleep, seeded RNG

END STATE, all four or the goal is not met:
  - the convention is written down in docs/, with the trade-off stated honestly (see DISSENT below)
  - the gates exist as engines on the standard contract and ride devtools.run
  - sdlc-devtools' own unit suite is CONVERTED -- this is the bulk of the work, not an afterthought
  - the gates BLOCK on sdlc-devtools, having been run against a converted tree

WHAT SHIPS TO CONSUMERS: the gates, green on a fresh generation (no code means nothing to violate, so a new
project ratchets from day one -- the shape_contracts precedent). EXISTING consumer repos are NOT converted
by us and are not blocked by us. Their agents decide adoption and send a ticket back if the convention does
not fit. Standing rule: the scaffold's job ends at the scaffold.

MEASURED STARTING POINT (2026-07-21, and it is why the conversion is the bulk of the work):
    naming compliance                    0%  in every tree -- not one test is named test_<method>
    parametrized tests                   1%  -- mean 1.4-1.8 asserts/test, 190 of devtools' 276 have one
    devtools methods with a mirror test  52/128 (41%) -- 76 violations, of which 48 are cross-module
                                                        contracts and 17 are public-by-accident
    devtools assert-half violations       0  -- already clean, which is what makes it the graduation tree
    unseeded RNG / external-data reads    0 in devtools; present in consumers

DISSENT, to be stated in the doctrine rather than hidden -- this convention is a deliberate MINORITY
position and the doctrine loses credibility if it pretends otherwise. Google's SWE book says "Test
behaviors, not methods" and "Test names should describe the behavior being tested"; Ian Cooper's trigger is
"a new behavior, not a new function"; Kent Beck lists structure-insensitivity as a desirable property.
The counter-argument, and the reason it is taken anyway: those positions optimise for refactoring velocity,
this one optimises for demonstrable completeness and traceability -- which is what the scaffold sells, and
what ASPICE SWE.4 asks for and no tool enforces. Beck frames his properties as sliders, not rules, so this
is a coordinate in that space rather than a violation of it.
Full sourcing: research/deep_dives/2026-07-21_test_architecture_sota.md
notes:
SCOPED TO UNIT, 2026-07-21 (owner's call). The conversation that produced this epic ranged across fixture
strategy, oracle sources and chain testing. Only the UNIT-level part is being ironed out; the rest is
recorded and parked.

Owner's reasoning, and it is the same rule already applied to consumers: "i am most experienced with unit
tests and feel i know what should be there. but people who worked on the other levels know them too." The
scaffold gates what its author has the expertise to gate. Integration and e2e doctrine waits for someone
with that experience rather than being invented here.

IN SCOPE -- unit only:
    c6b   phase 1  method mirror, CALL only, advisory
    phu   phase 2  the ASSERT half + per-method ignore
    ccy   phase 3  test_<method> naming + parametrized tables + doubles doctrine
  and adjacent, same family (properties a unit test must have):
    ar6   a tests/unit test must be SMALL -- no external data root, no network, no sleep
    1j3   unseeded RNG in unit tests

PARKED, recorded in research/deep_dives/2026-07-21_test_architecture_sota.md sections 3b and 4b:
  - per-JOINT contract testing (A's output is valid input to B). Already a stated house rule in CLAUDE.md,
    not gated, and integration-level.
  - END-TO-END invariant assertions over a canonical example.
  - the Standard-Fixture-flowing-through-all-levels idea. The UNIT half of it lands in ccy; the
    cross-level half is parked.
  - Google's SIZE axis beyond the unit tier (medium = localhost only, large = multi-machine).

The parked material is not rejected. It is un-gated because the expertise to gate it well is not here yet.
close_reason:
END STATE met, all four conditions:
  - the convention is written down in docs/UNIT_TESTS.md, WITH the dissent stated (section 5)
